name: Baked Empanadas
recipeIngredient: Cooked pinto beans (2 cups), rinsed and drained if canned
Green chilies (½ cup), chopped from can
Cheddar or Monterey Jack cheese (¾ cup), grated
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the Bean Filling: Coarsely mash the beans. Stir in the onion, garlic, green chilies, chili powder, ground cumin, and cider vinegar.
2. Make the Beef Filling: Sauté the lean ground beef, onion, and garlic in a skillet over medium heat until the meat is browned and vegetables are soft (about 5 minutes). Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in chopped green chilies, chili powder, ground cumin, and salt to taste.
3. Assemble the Empanadas: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Place a disk of dough on a work surface. Spoon about 2 tablespoons of bean filling onto one half, leaving a ½-inch border around the edge. Top with grated cheese if desired. Brush edges with water and fold dough over the filling to form a half circle. Press edges firmly together and poke holes in the top for steam release.
4. Repeat with meat filling, topping with salsa if desired. Place empanadas on a baking sheet and lightly coat with vegetable oil spray.
5. Bake for 15-20 minutes until golden brown. Serve hot or at room temperature.
Nutritional info per serving:
- Beef: 180 calories; 8g protein, 24g carbs, 6g fat (32%)
- Bean: 185 calories; 8g protein, 34g carbs, 6g fat (32%)
Source: Miami Herald, 2/15/96 format by Lisa Crawford, 7/7/96
